new_classes.label_en: undenatured ethyl alcohol of alcoholic strength ≥ 80 % vol (CN 2207 10)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von 80 % vol oder mehr, unvergällt (KN 2207 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) that has not been denatured and has an alcoholic strength by volume of 80 % vol or higher, as described in CN subheading 2207 10. Spirituous beverages (e.g. gin, vodka) falling under CN subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78 are explicitly excluded regardless of their alcoholic strength.

coverage_explanation: The three principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the substance is ethyl alcohol (new class as subclass of BFO Object), (2) it is undenatured (hasValue restriction on isDenatured = false), and (3) its alcoholic strength by volume is ≥ 80 % vol (decimalRange restriction and someValuesFrom AlcoholicStrengthByVolumeQuality). A complement restriction is added to express that spirituous beverages (SpiritousBeverage2208) are excluded. Coverage is not 1.0 because the exclusion of spirituous beverages is best expressed as a disjointness with the SpiritousBeverage2208 class rather than as a property complement restriction (OWL-DL cannot directly express 'is not an instance of class X' as a restriction in the standard way used here), and the cross-reference to specific HS Explanatory Notes (fourth paragraph exclusion) has no product-discriminating ontological content that can be further formalised.
